evals.name: new-sprint-scaffold
evals.prompt: I'm starting Sprint 11 tomorrow (May 13 2026). Goal is polish + hardening after the S10 skill-engine push — no new major features, just retire S10's carry-in debt and tighten the observability story. Velocity target 75 pts. Carry-in: S10-05 (Dev Console session-context tab polish) and S10-08 (modality-partial edge cases). Two weeks. Scaffold the tracker.
evals.expected_output: A new docs/SPRINT-11-tracker.md file with the meta block (dates May 13 -> May 27, goal, 75 pts, Status 🟡 Open), a Carry-in section naming S10-05 and S10-08, an Epic section (possibly TBD stub since bang didn't specify epics), a Sprint Summary table, and placeholder Key Decisions / Files Changed / Run Summaries sections. No invented velocity breakdowns.

assertions.text: File docs/SPRINT-11-tracker.md was created
Meta block contains 'Sprint dates:' line with 2026-05-13 and 2026-05-27
Meta block contains 'Velocity target:' line with 75
Status is 🟡 Open
Carry-in section mentions both S10-05 and S10-08
Sprint Summary table exists with Total row showing 75
Key Decisions, Files Changed, and Run Summaries sections are present as headers
No hallucinated story points assigned to stories bang didn't specify

evals.name: finish-sprint-run-summary
evals.prompt: We just shipped S10-12 (skill engine wiring + Dev Console Pipeline tab, 8 pts). It retires S9-07 (flaky Zod retry). Commits are in range f5f304e..f16186a. Tests went from 639 to 646 passing. Write the run summary — it touches HTTP API surface (/api/skill/execute, /dev/skills), has new Dev Console UI, and adds a skillEngine.enabled flag (default on).
evals.expected_output: A docs/sprint-runs/S10-12-<slug>.md file with the meta block filled in (Sprint 10, S10-12, 8 pts, Retires: S9-07, Landed: today, Status: ✅ Done), an opening paragraph linking prior runs, a What shipped list from the commit range, Architectural decisions with 'why X over Y' rationale, a Quick-start runbook with # expect: annotations, a Validation matrix split between sandbox ✅ and Mac 🟡, End-to-end Dev Console walkthrough section (since has-ui), a curl cheat sheet piped through jq (since has-http-api), a Feature-flag matrix row for skillEngine.enabled, a Debt retired section for S9-07, a What's next section, and Cross-references.

assertions.text: File docs/sprint-runs/S10-12-<anything>.md exists
Meta block has Sprint: 10, Story: S10-12 (8 pts), Retires: S9-07, Status: ✅ Done
Opening paragraph is 2-4 sentences and references context from prior runs
What shipped section lists actual files from git range (not hallucinated)
Architectural decisions section has at least 1 numbered entry with 'why X over Y' or equivalent trade-off framing
Quick-start runbook contains at least one `# expect:` annotation
Validation matrix contains both ✅ and 🟡 rows
Prerequisites bang must run on his Mac section is present
Dev Console walkthrough section is present
curl cheat sheet present with jq pipe and # expected: annotation
Feature-flag matrix has row for skillEngine.enabled
Debt retired section present with S9-07 named
What's next has 1-3 bullets, not an essay
No `<!-- FILL: ... -->` placeholders remain in the final output
Uses em-dashes (—) not double-hyphens (--) in prose

evals.name: sprint-index-rollup
evals.prompt: We've added three new run summaries since the last index build (S10-04, S10-05, S10-11, S10-12). Regenerate docs/sprint-runs/index.md so it picks up everything and sorts newest-first by Landed date.
evals.expected_output: A regenerated docs/sprint-runs/index.md with the preamble paragraph, a table (columns: Run, Story, Landed, Status, Summary), one row per existing run summary file (not just the new ones — all of them), sorted by Landed desc. The one-line summaries should be extracted from each run's opening paragraph (truncated to ~140 chars). Nothing else in the docs/ tree should be touched.

assertions.text: File docs/sprint-runs/index.md was written
Index contains the preamble paragraph explaining the ledger's purpose
Table has columns: Run, Story, Landed, Status, Summary
One row per S*.md file in docs/sprint-runs/ (excluding index.md itself)
Rows sorted newest-first by Landed date
Each Run cell is a markdown link to the corresponding file
No run summary files were modified (script only touches index.md)
One-line summaries truncated at ~140 chars where applicable
